task_completion.description: Verify the grayscale elevation raster is loaded into gvSIG Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG Desktop application is open, and a layer named 'elevation.tif' (or similar) is visible in the Table of Contents on the left side of the screen.

task_completion.description: Access the symbology or color table properties for the raster layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A properties or symbology dialog box is open for the elevation raster layer, showing options for color tables, color ramps, or raster rendering.

task_completion.description: Select a multi-color ramp (e.g., Rainbow, Relief, Precipitation) for the raster symbology.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A color ramp with multiple distinct colors is selected or actively being applied within the symbology dialog.

task_completion.description: Apply the chosen color ramp so the raster is displayed with multiple colors in the map view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main map view shows the raster layer rendered in multiple colors (not grayscale), matching the selected color ramp.

task_completion.description: Export the map view containing the colored raster as a PNG image to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog shows the map being saved as 'colored_elevation.png' in the '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/' directory, or a file manager/image viewer shows the final exported colorful PNG.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG Desktop GUI to apply the symbology and export the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with gvSIG Desktop menus, dialogs, and tools, rather than terminal commands executing GDAL or Python scripts to colorize and export the image.

integrity.description: The exported image is genuinely generated from the gvSIG map view.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export dialog in gvSIG is used, and the final PNG visually matches the colored raster seen in the gvSIG map view.

integrity.description: The agent did not write a script in a text editor to bypass the gvSIG GUI requirement.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are open with Python/bash scripts designed to process the TIFF and generate the PNG directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input raster is located at /home/ga/gvsig_data/elevation.tif. The expected output must be saved to /home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/colored_elevation.png. The output image must display multiple colors (minimum color variance of 2.0, ideally > 10.0) to confirm the pseudocolor symbology was successfully applied.
